title: Customer Support Knowledge Base Modernization
decision_needed: Approve discovery funding and portfolio priority for modernizing the customer support knowledge base and related content governance model.
decision_owner: Customer Operations leadership team with Finance and Technology review.
requested_action: Approve a 6-week discovery phase and authorize preparation of a full implementation funding request after discovery validates scope, cost, and integration assumptions.
problem_statement: Support agents rely on fragmented, outdated, and inconsistently governed knowledge sources, increasing handle time, rework, and customer frustration. Leaders need a decision on whether to invest in a governed knowledge base modernization effort before committing implementation funding.
strategic_alignment: The initiative supports operational efficiency, improved customer experience, faster onboarding, and lower support burden. It also creates a better foundation for automation and AI-assisted support without assuming automation benefits before content quality is improved.
current_state: Knowledge content exists across shared drives, wiki pages, ticket macros, and individual team notes. Ownership is unclear. Updates are inconsistent. Agents often escalate or search multiple sources before answering common issues.
gap_analysis: The organization lacks a single governed knowledge source, content ownership model, quality review cadence, and measurement approach for content usage and deflection. The gap is not only tooling; it includes governance, process, and accountability.
desired_future_state: Support teams use a governed knowledge base with accountable content owners, standard article formats, review cadences, usage analytics, and integration points for support workflow and future AI assistance.
scope_out: Enterprise-wide knowledge management beyond customer support

options.name: Do nothing
options.description: Maintain the current mix of shared drives, wiki pages, macros, and team notes.
options.notes: Useful as a baseline but does not address the operating problem.

options.name: Tool-only replacement
options.description: Select a new knowledge tool and migrate content with limited governance redesign.
disadvantages: May recreate current-state content quality problems

options.name: Governed modernization
options.description: Run discovery, define content governance, validate tooling needs, then fund implementation in phases.
disadvantages: Benefits depend on adoption and content ownership

recommended_solution: Approve a 6-week discovery phase for a governed knowledge base modernization effort, with a follow-on implementation business case after validating content inventory, ownership model, integration needs, costs, and measurable benefits.

benefits.name: Reduced agent search and rework
benefits.evidence: Initial support-manager notes indicate agents search multiple sources; baseline measurement still required.
benefits.measurement: Measure average handle time, escalation rate, duplicate ticket handling, and article usage before and after implementation.

benefits.name: Improved content quality and accountability
benefits.evidence: Current ownership is unclear across sources; discovery should validate ownership gaps.
benefits.measurement: Track article owner assignment, review currency, stale content, and content quality defects.

benefits.name: Better foundation for AI-assisted support
benefits.evidence: Assumption: governed content is prerequisite for reliable AI assistance.
benefits.measurement: Readiness score for content coverage, source trust, and review cadence.

costs.notes: Requires support operations, technology, finance, and content owner participation.
Discovery should compare reuse of existing tools vs. new platform.

risks.impact: Could fund a platform that does not solve the root problem.
risks.mitigation: Require governance and content ownership design before implementation funding.

risks.impact: Benefits may not materialize if agents continue using shadow sources.
risks.mitigation: Include training, search workflow integration, and leadership accountability.

assumptions.item: Current knowledge fragmentation materially affects support productivity.
assumptions.impact: Value case weakens if baseline data does not support productivity impact.
assumptions.validation: Measure handle time, escalation, and agent search behavior during discovery.

constraints.item: Implementation funding is not yet approved.
constraints.impact: Only discovery and business case maturation can proceed initially.
constraints.response: Limit initial decision request to discovery funding and prioritization.

governance: Use a weekly discovery checkpoint with Customer Operations, Technology, and Finance. Exit criteria should include validated current-state evidence, option comparison, cost model, risks, assumptions, implementation phases, and a clear funding decision request.
next_steps: Confirm decision owner and discovery budget range
Collect support metrics and content inventory
Identify technology constraints and existing platform options
Prepare discovery charter or lightweight initiation brief
open_questions: What is the baseline handle time or search burden?
Which current systems must integrate with the knowledge base?
What budget range is available for discovery and implementation?
Who will own content after launch?
